title: Analyze a solver's stability region
description: Determine the step-size range that keeps a solver bounded for the system's eigenvalues.
body: Use when choosing a step for an explicit solver on a known linear system. Do not pick a step empirically without understanding the stability boundary, since the failure is abrupt. Actions: estimate the system's fastest eigenvalue, compute the solver's stability limit on the step times that eigenvalue, and verify the chosen step stays inside it. Validation signals include a bounded solution at the chosen step and divergence appearing only once the step crosses the computed limit. Risk boundary: operating near the stability edge is fragile, so escalate to an implicit solver when the stable step is impractically small.
